sections.paragraph: Eze 16:10, 13 Eze 16:13, according to the Hebrews, silk, a garment of silk, LXX, τρίχαπτον, i.e. according to Hesych. τὸ βομβύκινον ὕφασμα. Jerome, “a garment so fine that it might seem as fine as the finest hair.” From the etymology (from מָשָׁה to draw), nothing else can be learned than that very fine threads are denoted.
